text.title: Logging Principles
text.summary: Structural rules for log format, levels, sensitive data, and context attachment.

rule.statement: Emit structured JSON logs in production; human-readable format only in development.
rule.rationale: JSON logs are parseable by aggregators (Datadog, Loki, CloudWatch) without regex. Human-readable logs are faster to scan interactively.

rule.statement: Attach correlation ID (request_id) to every log entry via context variable, not per-call argument.
rule.rationale: Per-call arguments are forgotten; a context variable propagates automatically through the call stack once set at request entry.

rule.statement: Use DEBUG for diagnostic data only active during development; INFO for normal business events; WARNING for recoverable anomalies; ERROR for failures requiring investigation; CRITICAL for system-stopping conditions.
rule.rationale: Treating INFO as the default for everything drowns signal in noise and raises alerting thresholds unnecessarily.

rule.statement: Never log passwords, tokens, API keys, credit card numbers, SSNs, or full PII. Mask before writing using a structlog processor or Pino redact config.
rule.rationale: Log aggregators are often less secured than the primary database. A leaked log dump becomes a credential dump.

rule.statement: Do not log inside tight loops or hot paths. Log the aggregate result after the loop completes.
rule.rationale: Each log call serializes a JSON object and may involve I/O. Inside a loop processing 100k items this dominates execution time.
